timeline.label: FY2025 OTC annual discloses >1.3M mineral acres and 36,705 leased acres; insider table shows Mai 29.6% and Speron 11.6%.
H1 2025 lease income rises to $189,101 versus $149,310 prior year; net loss ($51,156) as overhead persists.
FY2024 mineral lease income $349,700; book value ~$14.3M (~$12.70 per share); cash ~$4M against ~$160K annual burn.
Company repurchases 51,633 shares at $27 and acquires 667,300 gross mineral acres (~$1.52 per acre), expanding dominant UP mineral position.
Mineral exchange with Michigan DNR reshapes leased acreage; most acquired acreage had been under state lease.
Timberland sale and partial liquidation distribute roughly $100 per share in two installments; company retains mineral estate.
Shareholders approve plan of partial liquidation; Keweenaw becomes pure-play Michigan/Wisconsin mineral lessor.
Highland Copper assumes Copperwood mining lease; escalating lease payments and net-smelter royalty if production begins.

industry.structure: Fragmented North American mineral-rights ownership with Keweenaw dominant on acreage in Michigan's Upper Peninsula; lessees (Highland Copper) carry development and operating risk.
industry.share_shift: Keweenaw gained >667K acres in 2024 at low per-acre cost; Highland Copper advances Copperwood while broader UP mining faces permitting and environmental scrutiny.
industry.trend: Copper demand tied to electrification and data-center build-out supports royalty option value, but Michigan political and environmental hurdles delay production timelines.
